ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general choux pastry class (FOODON_03301521); no specific durian puff entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated durian puff code. A0FKP (choux pastry / cream puff category) is the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for durian puff or choux pastry at the required specificity level.

main_ingredients: choux pastry shell (flour, butter, eggs, water)
durian-flavoured cream filling (durian pulp or durian flavouring, whipping cream, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard durian puff weighs approximately 60 g and is typically consumed as a single-piece snack or dessert item.

nutrient_source.title: Durian Puff — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Durian Puff (cr_id F11110012). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the known composition of choux pastry with whipping cream filling using USDA SR Legacy reference values for cream and butter, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: Choux pastry / cream puff — analogous GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database reference for cream-filled pastry products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for durian puff exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 49 (low) is estimated from the closest analogous cream-filled choux pastry entry in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (49 × 13.74) / 100 ≈ 6.7, based on a 60 g serving containing approximately 13.74 g carbohydrate. Input record cited GI 49 / GL 11.2 (per 100 g basis); GL per serving recalculated for a 60 g portion. Confidence 0.818 as stated in source record.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 229 mg/100 g with high saturated fat (~9.2 g/100 g) and low potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit durian puff consumption to one piece occasionally, as the borderline sodium content (229 mg/100 g) combined with high saturated fat from butter and whipping cream contributes to arterial stiffness and cumulative sodium load when multiple pieces are consumed.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 22.9 g/100 g with sugar 9.1 g/100 g, low GI 49, but high fat prolongs postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a low GI of 49, people with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict durian puff to a single small piece infrequently, as the combination of 22.9 g carbohydrate, 9.1 g sugar, and 16.1 g fat per 100 g creates a prolonged postprandial glucose response that impairs glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.2 g/100 g from butter and whipping cream, with possible trace trans fat from pastry preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Durian puff is high in saturated fat (estimated ~9.2 g/100 g), which raises LDL cholesterol and increases cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only and verify trans fat content on product packaging.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~9.2 g/100 g exceeds 5 g/100 g RA threshold; refined carbohydrate promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high saturated fat content from cream and butter (estimated ~9.2 g/100 g) exceeds the recommended threshold for rheumatoid arthritis management and may stimulate TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; durian puff should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with active RA.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Durian puff (榴莲泡芙) is a popular Singaporean snack and dessert consisting of a light, hollow choux pastry shell filled with a rich durian-flavoured cream. The choux pastry is made from a cooked dough of flour, butter, eggs, and water that is piped and baked until puffed and golden, producing a crisp exterior and airy interior. The filling is typically made from fresh durian pulp blended with whipping cream and sugar, or from durian-flavoured pastry cream, delivering the fruit's characteristic pungent, custard-like flavour and creamy texture. Per 100 g, durian puff provides 255 kcal, 4.7 g protein, 16.1 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~9.2 g), and 22.9 g carbohydrate with 9.1 g sugar and 2.6 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 229 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 49 (low), though the high fat content prolongs postprandial glucose absorption. Clinically, the high saturated fat content raises concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management, while the moderate carbohydrate and sugar load requires caution for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is borderline for hypertension. Durian puff is widely sold at Singaporean bakeries, dessert shops, and pasar malam (night markets), and is especially popular as a festive or celebratory treat. It is not suitable for vegans due to dairy and eggs, and contains gluten allergens.

Is durian puff high in sugar?
What is the GI of durian puff?

What is durian puff made of?
How much fat is in a durian puff?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11110012) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy reference values for cream and butter components, consistent with known choux pastry composition. GI estimated from closest analogous cream-filled pastry in University of Sydney GI Database at confidence 0.818. All mandatory multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine category.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA SR Legacy reference values for whipping cream and butter as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this value should be verified against product label when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for durian puff are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. 3. GI of 49 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cream-filled choux pastry; no direct measurement for durian puff exists. GL per serving recalculated for a 60 g portion (GL ≈ 6.7). 4. Trans fat presence from pastry butter is plausible but unconfirmed; product packaging should be checked. 5. Allergen classification includes durian as a tree nut category allergen per Singapore Food Agency guidance; individual sensitivity varies. 6. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule (g4) is not triggered.
